evals.prompt: Set up V8 coverage tracking for a Vitest project with branch coverage gating at 80% globally and a stricter threshold for the payments module
expected_patterns: src/payments/.*: { lines: .*, branches: .* } OR per-directory threshold for payments
reporter: [.*text.*lcov.*json-summary] OR reporter array with text, lcov, json-summary
anti_patterns: exclude entire payment module from coverage

evals.prompt: Configure Istanbul/nyc with .nycrc.json to enforce 80% branch coverage and prevent coverage regression in CI
Write a coverage ratchet script that prevents coverage regression by comparing current coverage to .coverage-ratchet.json and fails the build if metrics decrease
anti_patterns: allowing coverage to decrease without failing
hardcoded threshold instead of reading from ratchet file
not updating ratchet file on success

evals.prompt: Analyze coverage gaps in a project by parsing coverage-summary.json, sorting files by uncovered line count, and identifying which gaps are critical vs low-risk
expected_patterns: payment OR auth OR validation (critical paths)
anti_patterns: all gaps treated as equal priority
no distinction between critical and low-risk code
ignoring branch coverage in the analysis

evals.prompt: Explain why a function with 100% line coverage but 50% branch coverage is not fully tested, and how to fix it
expected_patterns: one branch only OR isPremium = true only
expect both true and false paths OR expect(discount(100, false))
line coverage does not guarantee branch coverage
anti_patterns: not testing both branches of a conditional
relying only on coverage % without branch awareness

evals.prompt: Configure Python test coverage with pytest-cov and coverage.py using pyproject.toml, setting branch=true and fail_under=80, and excluding TYPE_CHECKING and test files
Identify files to exclude from coverage calculations (barrel exports, generated code, type definitions) and justify why they should not count toward the project's coverage percentage
expected_patterns: does not add test value OR inflates denominator
anti_patterns: excluding error handlers to inflate coverage
excluding auth code because it is hard to test
excluding any business logic from coverage

evals.prompt: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs coverage on every PR, posts a coverage diff comment showing changed files, and fails the build if coverage drops below the ratchet threshold
expected_patterns: npm run test:coverage OR vitest run --coverage
anti_patterns: hardcoded coverage threshold in CI without ratchet
not showing diff coverage to reviewers
only running coverage on main branch

evals.prompt: Explain the difference between code coverage and mutation testing, and describe when mutation testing should be used (focusing on which code is worth mutating)
expected_patterns: mutation testing OR Stryker OR mutmut
pure business logic OR validators OR calculators
critical paths OR payment OR auth
anti_patterns: running mutation testing on entire codebase
no mention of incremental or targeting

evals.prompt: Justify why 100% coverage is usually wrong, and define a meaningful coverage target for a project with payment processing and utility functions
